title: Crosshair Customization Guide
description: Master crosshair settings to improve your aim. Learn popular pro player crosshairs, console commands, and customization tips.
heading: Crosshair Customization Guide
intro: Your crosshair is your primary visual guide for aiming accuracy. The right crosshair setting can significantly improve your performance by providing clear feedback on accuracy and weapon spread. This guide covers customization options, popular pro settings, and how to find your optimal crosshair.

faqs.question: What crosshair settings do pro players use?
faqs.answer: Most pros use small, static crosshairs with minimal spread. Popular configs include players using 1-2px gap with 2-3px thickness. The best crosshair is one that feels comfortable for your playstyle.

faqs.question: Should I use a dynamic or static crosshair?
faqs.answer: Competitive players typically use static crosshairs because they don't expand during movement. This provides consistent aiming feedback regardless of weapon accuracy.

faqs.question: How do I copy a pro player's crosshair?
faqs.answer: Use a crosshair generator tool with pro player configs, or manually enter console commands from their config files. Many community resources compile popular pro crosshairs.

faqs.question: What's the best crosshair size?
faqs.answer: There's no universal "best" size. Start with small (outline 0-1, size 1-2) and adjust based on your monitor size and distance. You should be able to see enemies clearly through it.

faqs.question: Can I import crosshairs from CS:GO?
faqs.answer: CS2 uses the same crosshair command structure as CS:GO, so most crosshair settings are directly transferable. You'll likely want to fine-tune slightly for your preference.
